metadata.summary: CodeQL Code Scanning on every PR as a required merge gate. Copilot Autofix proposes AI patches as reviewable suggestions — never auto-merged. AI detections cover Bash, Dockerfile, Terraform.

content.title: CodeQL + Copilot Autofix as the PR-time SAST Gate
content.summary: Every GitHub repository enables Code Scanning with CodeQL on `push` and `pull_request`. PRs that introduce a new CodeQL alert MUST be blocked from merge by a required check. Copilot Autofix runs automatically on alerts and posts an AI-generated patch as a PR suggestion that a human accepts, edits, or rejects — never auto-merge. For ecosystems CodeQL does not cover (Bash, Dockerfile, Terraform/HCL, PHP), enable AI-powered detections in the same Code Scanning pipeline so the gate is uniform across the stack.

why.p: CodeQL is a semantic dataflow engine, not a regex scanner: it follows tainted user input through control flow and catches injection, SSRF, deserialization and auth-bypass classes that pattern matchers miss. Copilot Autofix (GA 2024, expanded with hybrid AI detections in 2026) covers more than 90% of alert types in JavaScript, TypeScript, Java and Python and resolves over two-thirds of findings with little or no editing — GitHub's own measurements report median time-to-fix dropping from 1.5 hours manual to about 28 minutes when Autofix is enabled. The PR-time gate beats nightly scans because the diff is fresh, the author is paged, and the suggestion lands inside the review the human is already doing.

list.item: Any GitHub-hosted repository with executable code (public OSS gets it free; private repos need GitHub Advanced Security or Copilot Enterprise).
Any repo with HTTP, auth, deserialization, SQL/NoSQL, or template rendering surface.
Polyglot repos where shell, Dockerfile, Terraform or PHP also need SAST coverage via the AI detections lane.
Agent-driven feature work — agents read SARIF via `gh api` and treat new alerts as merge blockers.
Repositories not hosted on GitHub — use Semgrep or SonarQube instead; the Autofix loop is GitHub-specific.
Pure documentation, asset, or notebook repositories with no executable surface — overhead exceeds value.
Throwaway experimental branches that will never be merged — do not waste minutes on Code Scanning runs there.
Mass-renaming refactors that legitimately churn thousands of lines — schedule a one-shot baseline reset, not per-PR Autofix.

section.name: pr-blocking-gate
section.p: Code Scanning produces SARIF that GitHub turns into per-PR check runs. Branch protection on the default branch lists those check runs as required. Without the required-check wiring, alerts surface but do not block merge — the gate becomes advisory and stops working.

rule.statement: Every protected branch MUST list the CodeQL check name (for example `CodeQL / Analyze (javascript-typescript)`) as a required status check, and the CodeQL workflow MUST run on `pull_request` for every language matrix entry. A PR introducing one or more new CodeQL alerts MUST fail the check until the alert is dismissed-with-reason or fixed in the same PR.
rule.rationale: If the check is not required, a PR can be merged with red alerts and the gate is theatre. GitHub's own Advanced Security guidance ties the merge decision to the SARIF diff between base and head — that diff has to be the source of truth for the bot, the human reviewer, and the agent.

example.code: on: push: branches: [main] pull_request: branches: [main] jobs: analyze: uses: github/codeql-action/.github/workflows/codeql.yml@v3
example.note: Workflow runs on PR events; the resulting check can be required in branch protection.

example.code: on: schedule: - cron: '0 6 * * 1' # Monday morning only
example.note: A weekly scheduled scan never produces a per-PR check — there is nothing to require, and bad code lands during the week.

antipattern.description: Marking the CodeQL check as "informational" because the team is overwhelmed by alerts during onboarding.
antipattern.reason: Once the gate is advisory, both humans and agents stop reading it; baseline the existing alerts as "won't fix" once, then keep the gate hard for everything new.

section.name: autofix-suggestion-loop
section.p: Autofix proposes a patch attached to the alert. The patch is treated as a draft fix: it must be opened in the diff, reasoned about, and either committed via the PR suggestion API or rewritten by the author. The merge bot does not consume Autofix suggestions automatically.

rule.statement: An agent or human MUST treat each Copilot Autofix suggestion as a draft patch: read the alert's data-flow explanation, run the test suite locally with the patch applied, and only then apply via "Commit suggestion" or rewrite. Autofix patches MUST NOT be auto-applied by a bot, and MUST NOT be merged without at least one non-author approval.
rule.rationale: Autofix achieves more than two-thirds correctness on common alert types but the remaining third either misses a constraint (locale, framework version, custom wrapper) or shifts the bug rather than fixing it; the reviewable-suggestion contract keeps the human or agent in the loop while still cutting median time-to-fix by roughly five times.

example.code: gh api -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \ /repos/$OWNER/$REPO/code-scanning/alerts/$ALERT_ID > alert.json gh pr checkout $PR git apply <(jq -r '.most_recent_instance.fix.diff' alert.json) pytest -x gh pr review --approve
example.note: Agent fetches the alert + Autofix patch, applies locally, runs tests, only then surfaces for human approval.
An auto-merge runner on every Autofix suggestion lands incorrect patches and silently rewrites security-sensitive code without review.

antipattern.description: Dismissing Autofix alerts as "false positive" without recording a reason, just to clear the queue.
antipattern.reason: Dismissals without `--reason` poison the audit trail; pick `false-positive`, `won-t-fix`, or `used-in-tests` so future scans and compliance reviews can replay the decision.

template.purpose: Minimal GitHub Actions workflow enabling CodeQL on push and PR with autobuild and SARIF upload.
Required-status-check JSON snippet for the `gh api` call that wires CodeQL into branch protection.

see-also.link: GitHub: about Code Scanning with CodeQL.
GitHub: AI-powered detections expanding Code Scanning coverage.
GitHub: responsible use of Code Scanning Autofix.
Found means fixed: Copilot + CodeQL Autofix announcement.
